sigWerErrors.reason: 'Buenas días' is grammatically incorrect in Spanish; 'Buenos días' is the correct greeting. The substitution actually corrects the phrase, so the meaning is unchanged.
The only difference is the addition of a comma, which is ignored per the guidance.
Changing 'hacer' (to do/make) to 'ser' (to be) alters the meaning of the sentence, as it changes the action being referred to in the context of future procedures.
